notes_md:
**bùng nổ** = to explode, or to suddenly grow very fast and strongly. It can be physical (like a bomb) or emotional/social (feelings, trends, crowds).

It’s a verb, often used for emotions, reactions, markets, technology, population, etc. — things that suddenly increase or release energy.  

It also means “boom” in English, like an economic or media boom.


Close synonyms: **nổ** (more literal explosion), **bùng phát** (used more for disease or problems spreading quickly).
exampleSentencesTranslated: When hearing the good news, I felt my emotions explode.
After his team won, the whole stadium exploded.
The big sale event made shopping demand explode nationwide.
notes:
bùng nổ = to explode, or to suddenly grow very fast and strongly. It can be physical (like a bomb) or emotional/social (feelings, trends, crowds).

It’s a verb, often used for emotions, reactions, markets, technology, population, etc. — things that suddenly increase or release energy.  

It also means “boom” in English, like an economic or media boom.

Close synonyms: nổ (more literal explosion), bùng phát (used more for disease or problems spreading quickly).

definitions.en: A sudden and intense increase or explosion.
definitions.vi: Một sự gia tăng hoặc bùng nổ đột ngột và mạnh mẽ.
definitions.es: Un aumento o explosión repentina e intensa.

example.en: The outbreak of the disease has caused everyone to worry.
example.vi: Sự bùng nổ của dịch bệnh đã khiến mọi người lo lắng.
example.es: El estallido de la enfermedad ha causado preocupación en todos.

exampleSentences: Khi nghe tin vui, em cảm thấy cảm xúc như muốn bùng nổ.
Sau khi đội bóng của anh chiến thắng, cả sân vận động đã bùng nổ.
Sự kiện giảm giá lớn khiến nhu cầu mua sắm bùng nổ trên toàn quốc.
